content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be reengineered to sort and retain environmental exploration data based on the intrinsic curiosity signals that power curious RL agents, creating a self-curating memory that prioritizes high-uncertainty, learning-driving experiences over passive or redundant observations. This curiosity-aligned memory architecture lets active learning agents avoid catastrophic forgetting of high-value exploration data that would otherwise be overwritten by common first-in-first-out or similarity-based memory pruning methods.
